data.title: Dow Jones, S&P 500, Nasdaq end at day's high ahead of crucial jobs report
data.description: Benchmark indices on Wall Street closed with strong gains on Thursday, September 4, ahead of an all important jobs report, which will more or less cement whether or not the US Federal Reserve cuts interest rates during its policy meeting on September 16-17.The Dow Jones ended with gains of over 350 points, the S&P 500 was higher by 0.8% and closed above 6,500, while the Nasdaq registered gains of close to 1%. All three benchmark indices closed at the day's high.A slew of macro data reported on Thursday indicated that the US labor market continues to cool, a point highlighted by Fed Chair Jerome Powell during his Jackson Hole address last month. Private payroll figures reported by the ADP showed hiring figures to be 54,000, below estimates and nearly half of the July figures.Hiring plans of US companies also fell to the lowest in any August since data is being tracked from 2009, as per Challenger, Gray & Christmas. Initial jobless claims increased by 8,000 from the prior week to 2,37,000, the highest since June. Trade deficit also widened as imports rose in July ahead of Donald Trump's reciprocal tariffs took effect.Post all of this macro data, the probability of the US Central Bank cutting interest rates in two weeks from now, stood at 99.4%, according to the CME FedWatch tool.Estimates for today's non-farm payrolls data are for an addition of 75,000 and will be the first figures reported under the new Bureau of Labor Statistics chief EJ Antoni, who was nominated by President Trump after he fired the previous commissioner last month over extreme downward revisions made to the May and June figures.Elsewhere, the US Dollar index continues to remain above the mark of $98, Brent Crude remains around $67 ahead of the OPEC+ meet over the weekend, while Gold prices remain at elevated levels on hopes of an interest rate cut.

data.title: Tourism Holdings Upgraded to Overweight from Market Weight by Wilsons
Tourism Holdings Price Target Raised 39% to A$2.94/Share by Wilsons
Demand for EVs Drives Auto Industry Sales — WSJ
data.description:
By Christopher Otts

Automakers are holding prices steady despite absorbing billions in tariff costs. Meanwhile, electric vehicle sales are surging, with consumers rushing to lock in expiring EV tax credits.

New-vehicle sales rose 3.7% in August, as consumers took advantage of the $7,500 federal tax credits ending Sept. 30. Prices, which had surged in the pandemic, are up only 1.5% so far this year, even after President Trump's tariffs began taking effect in spring and summer, according to J.D. Power.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Lululemon Seen With Further Pain Ahead — Market Talk
data.description: Lululemon's declining U.S. business and top-line miss in its latest quarter set the company up for a bumpy road ahead, Jefferies analysts say in a note. With a higher fixed cost base from years of building bigger stores, as well as peak margins, the activewear maker will likely continue to miss on earnings estimates going forward, and could lower its outlook again, the analysts say. "We believe guidance cuts continue and will become more severe through the end of the fiscal year," they say, reiterating their underperform rating on the stock. Shares fall 16%, to $173.97, in after-hours trading. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1914 ET [Dow Jones]--Lululemon's declining U.S. business and top-line miss in its latest quarter set the company up for a bumpy road ahead, Jefferies analysts say in a note. With a higher fixed cost base from years of building bigger stores, as well as peak margins, the activewear maker will likely continue to miss on earnings estimates going forward, and could lower its outlook again, the analysts say. "We believe guidance cuts continue and will become more severe through the end of the fiscal year," they say, reiterating their underperform rating on the stock. Shares fall 16%, to $173.97, in after-hours trading. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1902 ET - Sky Network Television's recent acquisition of Discovery NZ is both strategically smart and positive for earnings, Morgan Stanley says. As a pay TV business, Sky faces the same structural headwinds as most global peers. But there's a distinction worth highlighting to investors. "The main one being, as a small market (total population 5.3 million) it doesn't make sense for every studio to do direct-to-consumer in New Zealand," analyst Andrew McLeod says. "The scale economics don't work." So, more content suppliers are choosing to partner with Sky than go it alone in New Zealand. The Discovery NZ deal is a good example of this, MS says. The bank raises its price target on Sky by 15%, to NZ$2.29/share. Sky is down 2.6% at NZ$3.05 today. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1853 ET - Charter Hall's latest fund launches highlight the Australian property manager's momentum, Morgan Stanley says. Charter Hall this week launched the fund-raising for two new unlisted property funds, both on its Direct platform, to wholesale investors. Analyst Simon Chan says the DCRF and WPS3 funds highlight Charter Hall's wide range of sources of inflows, which now include high net worth individuals as targets. "We do not believe either DCRF or WPS3 to be too material in the scheme of Charter Hall's now A$69.4 billion property assets under management, given the small ticket size (e.g. minimum investment just A$100,000)," MS says. "But this is another incremental positive." MS retains an "overweight" call on Charter Hall. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1845 ET [Dow Jones]--IAG's proposed acquisition of RAC Insurance is unlikely to proceed after the Australian competition regulator raised concerns about the deal, Morgan Stanley says. It estimates the combined share of IAG and RAC in Western Australia's motor and home insurance market could approach 50%. That is likely too high. "We think the debate starts to shift back to organic growth," says analyst Andrei Stadnik. IAG's top-line growth guidance for FY 2026 — when excluding benefits from acquisitions — is slightly softer than peers. "With less support from inorganic opportunities and fewer reinsurance levers to pull, we see IAG's earnings growth moderating alongside a slower pricing cycle," MS says. It has an equal-weight call on IAG. (david.winning@wsj.com)

1837 ET [Dow Jones]--Lotus Resources's A$65 million capital raise looks a sensible move to Ord Minnett. Lotus wants to fortify its balance sheet as it ramps up output from the Kayelekera uranium mine in Malawi. Analyst Matthew Hope had felt Lotus's finances were strong enough to manage the restart of Kayelekera. But he says Lotus is raising from a position of strength because its share price is up some 43% over the prior month. "We consider Lotus's approach prudent, as it will consume working capital in December half of 2025 with the commencement of mining and growth of product and run-of-mine inventories," Ord Minnett says. "Cash receipts are most likely due in June half of 2026." (david.winning@wsj.com)

1829 ET [Dow Jones]--Vault Minerals's surprise decision to buy back stock is a good "back-stop" mechanism for its share price, Euroz Hartleys says. Vault didn't put a dollar value on the buyback. It said the intention is to repurchase up to 10% of its share capital over 12 months. Analyst Mike Millikan notes that Vault's share price has risen some 36% to A$0.57 since the buyback was announced. He thinks Vault could spend A$180 million on repurchasing stock. Euroz Hartleys retains a buy call on Vault, and lifts its price target by 12% to A$0.75/share. (david.winning@wsj.com)

1752 ET - Australia's recent company reporting season hinted at improving consumer demand in Australia and New Zealand, Jefferies analysts write in a note. Parsing data from the past five weeks, Michael Simotas and Naveed Fazal Bawa observe that the period was volatile for stocks, but largely positive for those in consumer sectors. They say that 10 out of 13 companies they cover saw their shares outperform the broader market across the reporting season, with furniture retailers Harvey Norman and Nick Scali the best performers. They see further upside potential for Harvey Norman, Nick Scali and supermarket supplier Metcash, on the expectation that current consumer-demand trends will continue. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

1744 ET - Australia's S&P/ASX 200 looks set to extend its late-week rally after hopes of a Fed rate cut helped lift U.S. equities. ASX futures are up by 0.4% ahead of Friday's session, suggesting that the benchmark index will add to the 1.0% rise that trimmed its deficit so far this week to 1.6%. The ASX still looks almost certain to snap a run of four consecutive weekly gains. In the U.S., the S&P 500 gained 0.8% and closed at a record as the release of weaker employment data was seen as supporting the case for the Fed to cut later this month. The DJIA added 0.8% and the Nasdaq Composite put on 1.0%. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

1742 ET - Consumers are spending less on performance activewear, and apparel overall, as they grow more selective in their purchases, seeking out truly new styles, CEO Calvin McDonald says during a call with analysts. As a result, the company's aims to reinvigorate demand with new seasonal colors in its core product lineup hasn't played out as expected, he says, though customers have responded well to its entirely new styles. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1734 ET - Lululemon has relied too heavily on its core franchises, like its scuba sweatshirts, a move that has caused sales to slow as consumers seek new products, CEO Calvin McDonald says during a call with analysts. "We have let our product life cycles run too long within many of our core categories," he says, noting the company has become too predictable within its casual offerings, missing opportunities to create new trends. The company now plans to add more new items at a faster pace, with plans for new styles to make up 35% of its total assortment in the spring, up from 23% currently, he says. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1604 ET - U.S. stocks rise ahead of tomorrow's monthly payroll update. ADP's private-employment report shows fewer new jobs than expected in August, while weekly jobless claims are slightly higher than forecast. Markets are pricing in a rate cut later this month, largely tied to a weakening labor market. Consumer discretionary stocks climb the most, helped by some strong retailer earnings. All members of the Magnificent 7 stocks rise. DJIA gains 350 points, or 0.8%, to 45621, the S&P 500 rises 0.8% to 6502 and the Nasdaq climbs 1% to 21707. (patrick.sullivan@wsj.com)

1543 ET - Boeing says it could replace striking St. Louis-area machinists as the deadlock between the two sides moves into its second month. The aerospace company says contingency plans for its fighter-jet and munitions production lines are working, though "requisitions for permanent replacement workers" are being posted ahead of a virtual job fair Sept. 16. Roughly 3,200 IAM members walked off in early August over dissatisfaction with wage and benefits offers. The union on Thursday said "the ball is in Boeing's court" to restart contract talks. (andrew.fitzgerald@wsj.com; @drewfitzgerald)

data.title: Revolution Medicines CFO Sells 10,000 Shares
data.description:
Reporter NameAnders Jack
RelationshipChief Financial Officer
TypeSell
Amount$400,992
SEC FilingForm 4

Revolution Medicines' Chief Financial Officer, Anders Jack, sold a total of 10,000 shares of common stock on September 2 and 3, 2025, under a 10b5-1 trading plan. The sales were executed at weighted average prices of $40.0168 and $40.1742, respectively, totaling $400,992. Following these transactions, Jack directly owns 113,314 shares of Revolution Medicines, including 54,213 RSUs.

SEC Filing: Revolution Medicines, Inc. [ RVMD ] - Form 4 - Sep. 04, 2025

Alphatec Holdings CEO, Miles Patrick, sold 300,000 shares of common stock on September 2, 2025, for a total of $4,719,000. The shares were sold at a weighted average price of $15.73. Following the transaction, Patrick directly owns 5,564,078 shares and indirectly owns 260,900 shares through an IRA and MOM, LLC. The sales were made under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan adopted on June 3, 2025.

data.title: Trump Signs Order to Enact Lower Tariffs on Japanese Auto Imports
data.description:
US President Donald Trump signed an order on Thursday to implement the lower tariffs on Japanese automobile imports and other products that were announced in July, Reuters reported.

The move formalizes an agreement between the US and Tokyo following months of negotiations, reduces uncertainty for Japan’s massive auto sector, and confirms a commitment of USD 550 billion in Japanese investment in US projects.

The lower tariffs on Japanese autos are set to take effect seven days after the order is published, with some of the relief retroactive to August 7.

According to Reuters, citing a Japanese government source, the US tariff rate on Japanese cars will be reduced from the current 27.5% to 15% by the end of the month.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 7 PM ET: Stocks Rally as Hopes for Rate Cuts Build | Trump ...
data.description:
Stocks Rally as Hopes for Rate Cuts Build

S&P 500's gains lift broad benchmark to new record.

----

Trump aide chosen for Fed board clears first hurdle as questions swirl about central bank's independence

President Donald Trump's effort to remake the board of the Federal Reserve and push the central bank to cut interest rates gained momentum after a top White House adviser appeared to win the endorsement of Republican senators.

----

ADP says just 54,000 private jobs created in August. Businesses still hesitant to hire.

Privately run businesses created just 54,000 jobs in August, ADP said, extending a string of poor employment reports that have raised alarms about the labor market and are likely to spur the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates soon.

----

A big part of the economy grew faster in August, ISM finds, but not because everything is A-OK

The large services side of the U.S. economy grew at the fastest pace in six months in August, but the improvement was driven by efforts to beat tariff-related price increases as businesses prepared for the Christmas shopping season.

----

Jobless claims rise to highest level since June, adding to concerns about labor market

What worries policymakers is that the labor market tends to weaken quickly once layoffs commence.

----

Canada Trade Deficit Narrows as Exports Revive

July figure reflects tenuous recovery from the wallop of Trump tariffs earlier this year, economists say, though trade overall remains weak.

----

Trump Administration Seeks Swift Supreme Court Review on Tariffs

The Treasury secretary in a court submission said the lower-court loss is already hurting trade negotiations.

----

U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Post Unexpected Increase

U.S. crude oil inventories rose by 2.4 million barrels last week. Analysts had predicted stockpiles would fall by 2 million barrels.

----

U.K. Government's Debt Problems Might Not Be Worsened by Rise in Long-Term Yields

The U.K.'s government has struggled to halt a rise in its debt, but a recent increase in yields on its long-term bonds might not make its task much more difficult.

----

What to Look for in First Jobs Report Since Trump Fired BLS Chief

Economists and policymakers will be watching Friday's report closely to see if hiring continued to cool this summer.

----

Germany's Ifo Institute Cuts Growth Forecasts on Tariff Impact

The economy is set to grow by less than expected, as tariffs weigh and the government's planned fiscal loosening offers less of a boost to activity, leading economists said Thursday.

----

DOJ Opens Criminal Investigation Into Fed's Cook, Issues Subpoenas

The probe centers on whether the Fed governor submitted fraudulent information on mortgage applications.

----

Pump-and-Dump Worries Prompt Nasdaq to Tighten China Listing Rules

Some Wall Street veterans and investor-protection advocates have said such listings have become a hotbed for fraud and manipulation.

----

Chinese Stocks Pull Back After Red-Hot Rally

Equities swooned in both China and Hong Kong, led by semiconductor and tech hardware stocks, which had risen the most over the past weeks.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Company Headlines at 7 PM ET: Broadcom Swings to Profit, Beats on Sales Outlook | Record ...
data.description:
Broadcom Swings to Profit, Beats on Sales Outlook

The semiconductors and software maker surpassed expectations for fiscal third-quarter sales and its outlook for the current quarter.

----

Record EV Sales Drive Auto Market Ahead of Credit End

Automakers are holding prices steady despite tariffs shaving $12 billion and counting from their bottom lines.

----

Lululemon Cuts Outlook as U.S. Sales Underperform

The company said its updated guidance reflects lower-than-expected revenue in the second quarter, driven by the company's U.S. business, as well as higher tariff rates.

----

Rivian Lays Off Workers as It Preps Launch of Cheaper SUV

The EV maker had been facing pressure as the U.S. market outlook has cooled to electric-only cars and trucks.

----

UiPath Boosts Outlook After Swinging to Profit

The automation-software company raised its full-year revenue outlook after swinging to a profit in its latest quarter, helped by momentum with its agentic artificial intelligence offerings.

----

Docusign Raises Outlook as AI Tools Gain Traction With Bigger Customers

The electronic-signature company is attempting to win bigger customers for its new AI tool that summarizes and analyzes agreement documents, Chief Executive Allan Thygesen said.

----

Cerence AI Files Lawsuit Against Apple, Alleging Patent Infringement

The lawsuit alleges Apple used Cerence's technology in certain iPhones and iPads without seeking a license.

----

Smith & Wesson First-Quarter Loss Widens as Sales Slip

The gun maker posted a loss of $3.4 million, compared with a loss of $1.9 million in the same quarter a year earlier.

----

T. Rowe Stock Jumps After Goldman Agrees to Invest $1 Billion, Team Up on Private Funds

The agreement aims to give Goldman a bigger foothold in 401(k)s amid private-market push, and help T. Rowe stem fund outflows.

----

Amazon's Satellite Internet Venture Signs Up First Airline

JetBlue plans to use Kuiper satellite internet on 25% of its fleet, with some passengers gaining access to the service in 2027.

----

Paramount Calls Employees Back to Office Five Days a Week

The media company, which aims to cut more than $2 billion in costs, is offering severance for those who don't want to return full time.

----

Carlyle's AlpInvest Unit Banks $20 Billion for Secondary Deals

Secondary funds are growing exponentially larger as a need for liquidity persists among private-markets investors.

----

Ciena Stock Spikes 18% on Earnings and Plans to Lay Off Up to 5% of Workforce

The networking-equipment company announces it will be laying off 4% to 5% of its workforce.

----

Lowe's CEO Marvin Ellison Is Prepared for a Housing Rebound

The home-improvement chain is adding market share among contractors, and using AI to train associates and assist customers.

----

Gap's CEO Makes Push Into Cosmetics at Gap and Old Navy Stores

Old Navy will roll out new beauty products this fall, and Gap stores will add fragrances next year.

data.title: ChartWatch ASX Scans: Lynas Rare Earths, Brazilian Rare Earths, Metallium, Deep Yellow, Nexgen Energy, HOT Chili

Welcome to my ChartWatch Daily ASX Scans series. Here, I present scan lists based on my trend following technical analysis methodology. My goal is to alert you to the best uptrends and downtrends on the ASX.

Feel free to get your favourite AI to convert the tables below into lists you can upload to your favourite trading platform like TradingView. Then you'll be able to skip from chart to chart and quickly and easily see the best uptrends and downtrends on the ASX.

Some investors prefer to buy those stocks in strong uptrends, and avoid, sell, or short sell those stocks in strong downtrends – but how you use the lists is really up to you!

Note, many stocks in both lists have appeared there many times before. As long as they keep meeting my criteria – they'll keep appearing. But note, there won't be any notifications when they don't – so you'll have to do your own research on when a particular trend changes!

data.title: Sunoco LP Announces Pricing of Upsized Private Offering of Senior Notes
data.description:
DALLAS, Sept. 4, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Sunoco LP ("Sunoco") today announced that it has priced at 100% a private offering (this "offering") of 5.625% senior notes due 2031 in an aggregate principal amount of $1 billion (the "2031 notes") and 5.875% senior notes due 2034 in an aggregate principal amount of $900 million (the "2034 notes," and collectively with the 2031 notes, the "notes"). This offering was upsized from an initial offering size of $850 million aggregate principal amount of the 2031 notes and $850 million aggregate principal amount of the 2034 notes. The sale of the notes is expected to settle on September 18, 2025, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.

Sunoco intends to use the net proceeds from this offering (i) on the closing date (the "Effective Date") of Sunoco's acquisition of all of the issued and outstanding common shares of Parkland Corporation ("Parkland" and such acquisition, the "Parkland Acquisition"), together with the net proceeds of the previously announced private offering of its Series A Fixed-Rate Reset Cumulative Redeemable Perpetual Preferred Units (the "Preferred Equity Offering"), to fund a portion of the cash consideration for the Parkland Acquisition and related transaction costs, with the remaining proceeds, if any, to be used for general corporate purposes, and (ii) prior to the Effective Date, to temporarily reduce the borrowings outstanding under Sunoco's revolving credit facility and pay interest and fees in connection therewith. This offering is not contingent on the completion of the Parkland Acquisition or the Preferred Equity Offering, and neither the Parkland Acquisition nor the Preferred Equity Offering is conditioned on the completion of this offering.

If (i) the Parkland Acquisition has not been completed on or prior to May 5, 2026 (the "Special Mandatory Redemption Date"), or (ii) prior to the Special Mandatory Redemption Date, (a) the Arrangement Agreement, dated as of May 4, 2025, among Sunoco, Parkland and certain of their respective affiliates, is terminated or (b) Sunoco will not pursue the completion of the Parkland Acquisition or has determined in its sole discretion that the completion of the Parkland Acquisition cannot or is not reasonably likely to be satisfied by the Special Mandatory Redemption Date, the notes will be subject to a special mandatory redemption at a price equal to 100% of the initial issue price of the notes to be redeemed plus accrued and unpaid interest to, but excluding, the Special Mandatory Redemption Date.

This offering of the notes has not been registered under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the "Securities Act"), or any state securities laws and, unless so registered, the notes may not be offered or sold in the United States except pursuant to an exemption from, or in a transaction not subject to, the registration requirements of the Securities Act and applicable state securities laws. Sunoco plans to offer and sell the notes only to persons reasonably believed to be qualified institutional buyers pursuant to Rule 144A under the Securities Act and to non-U.S. persons in transactions outside the United States pursuant to Regulation S under the Securities Act.

This news release is neither an offer to sell nor a solicitation of an offer to buy the notes or any other securities and shall not constitute an offer to sell or a solicitation of an offer to buy, or a sale of, the notes or any other securities in any jurisdiction in which such offer, solicitation or sale is unlawful prior to the registration or qualification under the securities laws of any such state or jurisdiction.

About Sunoco LP

Sunoco LP is an energy infrastructure and fuel distribution master limited partnership operating in over 40 U.S. states, Puerto Rico, Europe, and Mexico with an extensive network of approximately 14,000 miles of pipeline and over 100 terminals for midstream operations. Sunoco's general partner is owned by Energy Transfer LP .

Forward-Looking Statements

This news release may include certain statements concerning expectations for the future that are forward-looking statements as defined by federal law, including without limitation statements regarding this offering. Such forward-looking statements are subject to a variety of known and unknown risks, uncertainties, and other factors that are difficult to predict and many of which are beyond management's control. An extensive list of factors that can affect future results are discussed in Sunoco's Annual Report on Form 10-K, any subsequently filed Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q and Current Reports on Form 8-K and other documents filed from time to time with the Securities and Exchange Commission. Sunoco undertakes no obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statement to reflect new information or events.

Contacts

Scott Grischow

Treasurer, Senior Vice President — Finance

(214) 840-5660, scott.grischow@sunoco.com

Brian Brungardt

Director — Investor Relations

(214) 840-5437, brian.brungardt@sunoco.com

SOURCE Sunoco LP

data.title: Sunoco LP Announces Pricing of Upsized Preferred Equity Offering
data.description:
DALLAS, Sept. 4, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Sunoco LP ("Sunoco") today announced the pricing of a private offering (this "offering") of 1.5 million of its 7.875% Series A Fixed-Rate Reset Cumulative Redeemable Perpetual Preferred Units (the "Series A Preferred Units") at an offering price of $1,000 per unit. Sunoco will receive gross proceeds of $1.5 billion from the sale of the Series A Preferred Units before deducting the initial purchasers' discount and other estimated offering expenses. This offering was upsized from an initial offering size of 1 million Series A Preferred Units. This offering is expected to settle on September 18, 2025, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.

Use of Proceeds

Sunoco intends to use the net proceeds from this offering (i) on the closing date (the "Effective Date") of Sunoco's acquisition of all of the issued and outstanding common shares of Parkland Corporation ("Parkland" and such acquisition, the "Parkland Acquisition"), together with the net proceeds of the previously announced private offering of its senior notes due 2031 in an aggregate principal amount of $1 billion (the "2031 notes") and senior notes due 2034 in an aggregate principal amount of $900 million (the "2034 notes," and collectively with the 2031 notes, the "notes," and such offering, the "Notes Offering"), to fund a portion of the cash consideration for the Parkland Acquisition and related transaction costs, with the remaining proceeds, if any, to be used for general corporate purposes, and (ii) prior to the Effective Date, to temporarily reduce the borrowings outstanding under Sunoco's revolving credit facility and pay interest and fees in connection therewith. This offering is not contingent on the completion of the Parkland Acquisition or the Notes Offering, and neither the Parkland Acquisition nor the Notes Offering is conditioned on the completion of this offering.

If (i) the Parkland Acquisition has not been completed on or prior to May 5, 2026 (the "Special Mandatory Redemption Date"), or (ii) prior to the Special Mandatory Redemption Date, (a) the Arrangement Agreement, dated as of May 4, 2025, among Sunoco, Parkland and certain of their respective affiliates, is terminated or (b) Sunoco will not pursue the completion of the Parkland Acquisition or has determined in its sole discretion that the completion of the Parkland Acquisition cannot or is not reasonably likely to be satisfied by the Special Mandatory Redemption Date, the Series A Preferred Units will be subject to a special mandatory redemption at a price equal to $1,000 per Series A Preferred Unit plus, in each case, an amount equal to all accumulated and unpaid distributions thereon to, but excluding, the Special Mandatory Redemption Date, whether or not declared.

Details on the Series A Preferred Units

Distributions on the Series A Preferred Units, which will be paid semi-annually on March 18 and September 18 each year beginning March 18, 2026, will accrue and be cumulative from and including the date of original issue to, but excluding, September 18, 2030 (the "First Reset Date"), at a rate of 7.875% per annum of the stated liquidation preference of $1,000. On and after the First Reset Date, distributions on the Series A Preferred Units will accumulate at a percentage of the $1,000 liquidation preference equal to an interest rate equal to the Five-Year U.S. Treasury Rate (as described in the offering memorandum relating to this offering), plus a spread of 4.230% per annum. The Series A Preferred Units are redeemable, in whole or in part, on one or more occasions, at Sunoco's option on or after the First Reset Date at a redemption price of $1,000 per Series A Preferred Unit, plus, in each case, an amount equal to all accumulated and unpaid distributions thereon to, but excluding, the date of redemption.

The Series A Preferred Units are not convertible into or exchangeable for any other securities of Sunoco and will have limited voting rights. The Series A Preferred Units may be redeemed at the option of Sunoco in certain circumstances.

This offering of the Series A Preferred Units has not been registered under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the "Securities Act"), or any state securities laws and, unless so registered, the Series A Preferred Units may not be offered or sold in the United States except pursuant to an exemption from, or in a transaction not subject to, the registration requirements of the Securities Act and applicable state securities laws. Sunoco plans to offer and sell the Series A Preferred Units only to persons reasonably believed to be qualified institutional buyers pursuant to Rule 144A under the Securities Act and to non-U.S. persons in transactions outside the United States pursuant to Regulation S under the Securities Act.

This news release is neither an offer to sell nor a solicitation of an offer to buy the Series A Preferred Units or any other securities and shall not constitute an offer to sell or a solicitation of an offer to buy, or a sale of, the Series A Preferred Units or any other securities in any jurisdiction in which such offer, solicitation or sale is unlawful prior to the registration or qualification under the securities laws of any such state or jurisdiction.

About Sunoco LP

Sunoco LP is an energy infrastructure and fuel distribution master limited partnership operating in over 40 U.S. states, Puerto Rico, Europe, and Mexico with an extensive network of approximately 14,000 miles of pipeline and over 100 terminals for midstream operations. Sunoco's general partner is owned by Energy Transfer LP .

Forward-Looking Statements

This news release may include certain statements concerning expectations for the future that are forward-looking statements as defined by federal law, including without limitation statements regarding this offering. Such forward-looking statements are subject to a variety of known and unknown risks, uncertainties, and other factors that are difficult to predict and many of which are beyond management's control. An extensive list of factors that can affect future results are discussed in Sunoco's Annual Report on Form 10-K, any subsequently filed Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q and Current Reports on Form 8-K and other documents filed from time to time with the Securities and Exchange Commission. Sunoco undertakes no obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statement to reflect new information or events.

Contacts

Scott Grischow

Treasurer, Senior Vice President — Finance

(214) 840-5660, scott.grischow@sunoco.com

Brian Brungardt

Director — Investor Relations

(214) 840-5437, brian.brungardt@sunoco.com

data.title: Chicago Board of Education to Sell $650 Million of GO Bonds
data.description:
By Stephen Nakrosis

The Chicago Board of Education is selling $650 million of unlimited tax general obligation bonds with proceeds slated to pay for the district's capital improvement program.

Money from the sale of the Series 2025 A bonds will be used for site improvements, fire alarm replacements and IT upgrades, according to documents posted Thursday on MuniOS. Funds may also be used for electrical and energy efficiency projects, masonry remediation, chimney stabilization, along with other projects. Proceeds will be used to pay issuance costs.

The bonds are expected to price on Sept. 11, and the sale is scheduled to close on Sept. 25.

Interest on the securities is exempt from federal taxes, but they are subject to state taxes. The securities are backed by the full faith and credit of the board, as well as a pledge of state aid revenue and pledged taxes, among other funds.

Interest on the bonds will be payable starting on June 1 and again on Dec. 1. The bonds will mature from 2047 through 2050.

At the start of the 2024-25 school year, the Chicago's public schools had 325,305 students enrolled at 634 schools, including district-run, charter, contract, and SAFE schools.

The bonds received a BB+ rating from S&P Global Ratings.

Loop Capital and PNC are listed as the offering's lead managers.

Write to Stephen Nakrosis at stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com

-0-

data.title: Grades weaken as WTI/Brent spread eases below $4/bbl
data.description:
U.S. crude grades eased on Thursday, dealers said, as West Texas Intermediate crude futures' discount to Brent narrowed to below $4 a barrel.

WTI Midland weakened 10 cents, while WTI at East Houston (WTC-MEH), also known as MEH, eased 5 cents.

West Texas Intermediate crude futures' discount to Brent (WTCLc1-LCOc1) narrowed to as little as $3.93 a barrel. A discount below $4 typically disincentivizes export demand.

U.S. crude oil inventories rose last week as refineries headed into maintenance season, while gasoline stocks fell ahead of the long Labor Day weekend, data from the Energy Information Administration showed on Thursday.

Eight OPEC+ members will consider further raising oil production at a meeting on Sunday, two sources familiar with the discussions said, as the group seeks to regain market share.

In refining news, Irving Oil's 320,000-barrel-per-day refinery in Saint John, New Brunswick, will begin a 30-day turnaround later this month, the company said in a press release on Thursday.

* Light Louisiana Sweet (WTC-LLS) for October delivery eased 25 cents to a midpoint of a $2.15 premium and was seen bid and offered between a $2.00 and $2.30 a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures

* Mars Sour (WTC-MRS) was flat at a midpoint of a 30-cent premium and was seen bid and offered between a 15-cent and 45-cent a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures

* WTI Midland (WTC-WTM) eased 10 cents to a midpoint of a $1.05 premium and was seen bid and offered between a 85-cent and $1.25 a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures

* West Texas Sour (WTC-WTS) eased 25 cents to a midpoint of a 20-cent discount and was seen bid and offered between a 25-cent and 15-cent a barrel discount to U.S. crude futures

* WTI at East Houston (WTC-MEH), also known as MEH, traded between a $1.15 and $1.55 a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures

* ICE Brent November futures fell 61 cents to settle at $66.99 a barrel on Thursday

* WTI October crude futures fell 49 cents to settle at $63.48 a barrel on Thursday

* The Brent/WTI spread (WTCLc1-LCOc1) narrowed 7 cents to last trade at minus $3.96, after hitting a high of minus $3.93 and a low of minus $4.04.

RelationshipCHAIRMAN, CHIEF INNOVATION OFF, 10% Owner
TypeSell

SEC FilingForm 4

Amar K. Goel, Chairman and Chief Innovation Officer of PubMatic and a 10% owner, sold 8,020 shares of Class A Common Stock on September 2, 2025, under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan adopted on June 3, 2025. The shares were sold at a weighted average price of $8.4502, with prices ranging from $8.34 to $8.58. Following the transaction, Goel directly owns 12,496 shares of PubMatic.
